This failed." what the hell is that??? [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AFC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe[[ja:穴穂部皇子]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = July 19, 587 | death_place = [[Awaji]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = Oanenokimi | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[Awaji]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. Hikoushi no Ōkimi |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}}

He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[Awaji]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. Hikoushi no Ōkimi |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}}

revision.text: {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} {{AFC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AFC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe[[ja:穴穂部皇子]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = July 19, 587 | death_place = [[Awaji]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = Oanenokimi | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[Awaji]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. Hikoushi no Ōkimi |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}}

#redirect[[en:Prince Anahobe]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]]

{{出典の明記|date=2015年6月}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 穴穂部皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[欽明天皇]]皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = <!-- 日本の皇族のみ --> | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = [[用明天皇]]2年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[587年]][[7月17日]]） | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = | 父親 = [[欽明天皇]] | 母親 = [[蘇我小姉君]] | 役職 = | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''穴穂部皇子'''（あなほべのみこ、生年不詳 - [[用明天皇]]2年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[587年]][[7月17日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[欽明天皇]]の[[皇子]]。[[聖徳太子]]の叔父でもある。表記は『[[日本書紀]]』によるが、『[[古事記]]』では三枝部穴太部王（さきくさべのあなほべのみこ）、別名を須売伊呂杼（すめいろど（[[皇后]]の同母弟か））とする。『日本書紀』[[欽明天皇]]紀では住迹（すみと（[[天皇]]の弟か））皇子、[[用明天皇]]紀では皇弟（すめいろど）皇子とも記している。
== 生涯 == 欽明天皇の[[皇子]]として誕生。母は[[蘇我稲目]]の娘・[[蘇我小姉君|小姉君]]。異母兄に[[敏達天皇]]と[[用明天皇]]など。異母姉に[[推古天皇]]。同母姉に[[穴穂部間人皇女]]（用明天皇妃、[[聖徳太子]]生母）、同母弟に[[崇峻天皇]]、同母兄に[[茨城皇子]]と[[葛城皇子]]。
穴穂部皇子は皇位を望んでおり、敏達天皇14年（[[585年]]）8月、兄・敏達天皇が[[崩御]]し、[[殯|殯宮]]で[[葬儀]]が行われた際に「何故に死する王に仕え、生きる王（自分）に仕えないのか」と憤慨した<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇14年8月15日条</ref>。ところが、同年9月に[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]・[[蘇我馬子]]の推す大兄皇子（[[用明天皇]]）が即位したため、これに対抗し[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]・[[物部守屋]]と結んだ。
用明天皇元年（[[586年]]）5月、炊屋姫（敏達天皇の皇后、後の[[推古天皇]]）を犯さんと欲し、殯宮に押し入ろうとした。これに対し先帝の寵臣[[三輪逆]]は門を閉じて拒み、穴穂部皇子は7度門前で呼んだが、遂に宮に入ることができなかった。穴穂部皇子は蘇我馬子と物部守屋に三輪逆は不遜であると相談し、馬子らはこれに同意。守屋は兵を率い（用明天皇の宮のある）磐余の池辺の地で三輪逆を包囲するが、三輪逆は逃れて炊屋姫の後宮に隠れた。しかし、密告により居所を知ると穴穂部皇子は三輪逆とその二人の子を殺すよう守屋に命じ、守屋は兵を率いて向かった。その後、報告を聞こうと守屋のもとへ赴こうとするが、これを知りかけつけた馬子と門前で出会い「王者は刑人に近づくべからず」と諫言されるが、穴穂部皇子は聞き入れようとしなかった。馬子は仕方なくついて行き、磐余に至ったところで再度諫言、穴穂部皇子もこれに従い、胡床に座り守屋を待ち、戻ってきた守屋から三輪逆を斬ったと報告を受けた（或本では、穴穂部皇子自身が射殺した、とある）。なお、馬子は「天下の乱は近い」と嘆くが、守屋は「汝のような小臣の知るところにあらず」と答えている<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇元年5月条</ref>。
用明天皇2年（[[587年]]）4月2日、用明天皇は病になり[[仏法]]を信奉したいと欲し群臣に諮った。排仏派の守屋は反対したが、崇仏派の馬子は詔を奉じて助けるべきとして、穴穂部皇子に[[僧]]の[[豊国 (僧)|豊国]]を連れて来させた<ref>『日本書紀』ではこの場面のみ穴穂部皇子を「皇弟（すめいろど）皇子」と記している。同じ名前は[[孝徳天皇]]の時代、皇后[[間人皇女]]の同母弟だった大海人皇子（後の[[天武天皇]]）にも用いられている。法師については「名をもらせり」と注しており、人名ではなく[[豊国]]の法師の意と見られる。</ref>。守屋は自分が推していた穴穂部皇子が[[法師]]を連れてきたことに大いに怒り睨みつけた。その後、守屋は群臣から命を狙われていると知らされて、別業の阿都（[[河内国]]）へ退いた。
同年4月9日、用明天皇は崩御した<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年4月9日条</ref>が、後嗣が定まらず皇位は一時的に空位となった。そのため同年5月、守屋は穴穂部皇子を天皇に立てようと思い、密使を皇子に送り、遊猟に出ると欺いて淡路へ来るよう願った<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年5月条</ref>。これに対し同年6月7日、蘇我馬子は炊屋姫を奉じて、[[佐伯丹経手|佐伯連丹経手]]、[[土師磐村|土師連磐村]]、[[的真噛|的臣真噛]]に速やかに穴穂部皇子と[[宅部皇子]]を誅殺するよう命じた。その日の夜半、佐伯連丹經手らは穴穂部皇子の宮を囲んだ。穴穂部皇子は楼を登ってきた衛士に肩を斬られると、楼から落ちて隣家へ走り入ったが、灯をかかげた衞士らによって探し出され殺害された<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年6月7日条</ref>。なお、翌8日には穴穂部皇子と仲が良かった宅部皇子も誅殺され<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年6月8日条</ref>、同年7月には馬子によって物部守屋も滅ぼされている<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年7月条</ref>。
== 登場作品 == * テレビドラマ ** [[蘇我馬子|怒涛日本史 蘇我馬子の陰謀]]（[[1966年]] [[毎日放送]] 演：[[仲谷昇]]） ** [[聖徳太子 (テレビドラマ)|聖徳太子]]（[[2001年]] [[日本放送協会|NHK]] 演：[[柄本明]]） * 漫画 ** [[日出処の天子]]（[[山岸凉子]]） ** 聖徳太子（[[池田理代子]]）

{{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} {{AFC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AFC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe[[ja:穴穂部皇子]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = July 19, 587 | death_place = [[Awaji]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = Oanenokimi | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[Awaji]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. Hikoushi no Ōkimi |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}}

{{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} {{AFC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AFC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe[[ja:穴穂部皇子]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = July 19, 587 | death_place = [[Awaji]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = Oanenokimi | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[Awaji]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. Hikoushi no Ōkimi |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[en:Draft:Prince Anahobe]]

{{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} {{AFC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AFC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe[[ja:穴穂部皇子]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = July 19, 587 | death_place = [[Awaji]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = Oanenokimi | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[Awaji]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[en:Draft:Prince Anahobe]]

This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[en:Draft:Prince Anahobe]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text: {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} {{AFC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AFC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = July 19, 587 | death_place = [[Awaji]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = Oanenokimi | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[Awaji]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[Prince Shōtoku]] *[[Emperor Sushun]] *[[Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[en:Draft:Prince Anahobe]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年6月}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 穴穂部皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[欽明天皇]]皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = <!-- 日本の皇族のみ --> | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = [[用明天皇]]2年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[587年]][[7月17日]]） | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = | 父親 = [[欽明天皇]] | 母親 = [[蘇我小姉君]] | 役職 = | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''穴穂部皇子'''（あなほべのみこ、生年不詳 - [[用明天皇]]2年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[587年]][[7月17日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[欽明天皇]]の[[皇子]]。[[聖徳太子]]の叔父でもある。表記は『[[日本書紀]]』によるが、『[[古事記]]』では三枝部穴太部王（さきくさべのあなほべのみこ）、別名を須売伊呂杼（すめいろど（[[皇后]]の同母弟か））とする。『日本書紀』[[欽明天皇]]紀では住迹（すみと（[[天皇]]の弟か））皇子、[[用明天皇]]紀では皇弟（すめいろど）皇子とも記している。
== 生涯 == 欽明天皇の[[皇子]]として誕生。母は[[蘇我稲目]]の娘・[[蘇我小姉君|小姉君]]。異母兄に[[敏達天皇]]と[[用明天皇]]など。異母姉に[[推古天皇]]。同母姉に[[穴穂部間人皇女]]（用明天皇妃、[[聖徳太子]]生母）、同母弟に[[崇峻天皇]]、同母兄に[[茨城皇子]]と[[葛城皇子]]。
穴穂部皇子は皇位を望んでおり、敏達天皇14年（[[585年]]）8月、兄・敏達天皇が[[崩御]]し、[[殯|殯宮]]で[[葬儀]]が行われた際に「何故に死する王に仕え、生きる王（自分）に仕えないのか」と憤慨した<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇14年8月15日条</ref>。ところが、同年9月に[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]・[[蘇我馬子]]の推す大兄皇子（[[用明天皇]]）が即位したため、これに対抗し[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]・[[物部守屋]]と結んだ。
用明天皇元年（[[586年]]）5月、炊屋姫（敏達天皇の皇后、後の[[推古天皇]]）を犯さんと欲し、殯宮に押し入ろうとした。これに対し先帝の寵臣[[三輪逆]]は門を閉じて拒み、穴穂部皇子は7度門前で呼んだが、遂に宮に入ることができなかった。穴穂部皇子は蘇我馬子と物部守屋に三輪逆は不遜であると相談し、馬子らはこれに同意。守屋は兵を率い（用明天皇の宮のある）磐余の池辺の地で三輪逆を包囲するが、三輪逆は逃れて炊屋姫の後宮に隠れた。しかし、密告により居所を知ると穴穂部皇子は三輪逆とその二人の子を殺すよう守屋に命じ、守屋は兵を率いて向かった。その後、報告を聞こうと守屋のもとへ赴こうとするが、これを知りかけつけた馬子と門前で出会い「王者は刑人に近づくべからず」と諫言されるが、穴穂部皇子は聞き入れようとしなかった。馬子は仕方なくついて行き、磐余に至ったところで再度諫言、穴穂部皇子もこれに従い、胡床に座り守屋を待ち、戻ってきた守屋から三輪逆を斬ったと報告を受けた（或本では、穴穂部皇子自身が射殺した、とある）。なお、馬子は「天下の乱は近い」と嘆くが、守屋は「汝のような小臣の知るところにあらず」と答えている<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇元年5月条</ref>。
用明天皇2年（[[587年]]）4月2日、用明天皇は病になり[[仏法]]を信奉したいと欲し群臣に諮った。排仏派の守屋は反対したが、崇仏派の馬子は詔を奉じて助けるべきとして、穴穂部皇子に[[僧]]の[[豊国 (僧)|豊国]]を連れて来させた<ref>『日本書紀』ではこの場面のみ穴穂部皇子を「皇弟（すめいろど）皇子」と記している。同じ名前は[[孝徳天皇]]の時代、皇后[[間人皇女]]の同母弟だった大海人皇子（後の[[天武天皇]]）にも用いられている。法師については「名をもらせり」と注しており、人名ではなく[[豊国]]の法師の意と見られる。</ref>。守屋は自分が推していた穴穂部皇子が[[法師]]を連れてきたことに大いに怒り睨みつけた。その後、守屋は群臣から命を狙われていると知らされて、別業の阿都（[[河内国]]）へ退いた。
同年4月9日、用明天皇は崩御した<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年4月9日条</ref>が、後嗣が定まらず皇位は一時的に空位となった。そのため同年5月、守屋は穴穂部皇子を天皇に立てようと思い、密使を皇子に送り、遊猟に出ると欺いて淡路へ来るよう願った<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年5月条</ref>。これに対し同年6月7日、蘇我馬子は炊屋姫を奉じて、[[佐伯丹経手|佐伯連丹経手]]、[[土師磐村|土師連磐村]]、[[的真噛|的臣真噛]]に速やかに穴穂部皇子と[[宅部皇子]]を誅殺するよう命じた。その日の夜半、佐伯連丹經手らは穴穂部皇子の宮を囲んだ。穴穂部皇子は楼を登ってきた衛士に肩を斬られると、楼から落ちて隣家へ走り入ったが、灯をかかげた衞士らによって探し出され殺害された<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年6月7日条</ref>。なお、翌8日には穴穂部皇子と仲が良かった宅部皇子も誅殺され<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年6月8日条</ref>、同年7月には馬子によって物部守屋も滅ぼされている<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年7月条</ref>。
== 登場作品 == * テレビドラマ ** [[蘇我馬子|怒涛日本史 蘇我馬子の陰謀]]（[[1966年]] [[毎日放送]] 演：[[仲谷昇]]） ** [[聖徳太子 (テレビドラマ)|聖徳太子]]（[[2001年]] [[日本放送協会|NHK]] 演：[[柄本明]]） * 漫画 ** [[日出処の天子]]（[[山岸凉子]]） ** 聖徳太子（[[池田理代子]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[宇治谷孟]]『日本書紀 （下）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1988年 * 『日本書紀』（四）（[[岩波文庫]]、1995年）
== 関連項目 == * [[丁未の乱]] * [[穴穂部]]
{{デフォルトソート:あなほへのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:暗殺された日本の皇族]] [[Category:欽明天皇の子女]] [[Category:6世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:587年没]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[穴穂部皇子]] to [[Prince Anahobe]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年6月}} {{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 穴穂部皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[欽明天皇]]皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = <!-- 日本の皇族のみ --> | 出生日 = 不明 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = [[用明天皇]]2年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[587年]][[7月17日]]） | 没地 = | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = | 配偶者1 = | 子女 = | 父親 = [[欽明天皇]] | 母親 = [[蘇我小姉君]] | 役職 = | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''穴穂部皇子'''（あなほべのみこ、生年不詳 - [[用明天皇]]2年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[587年]][[7月17日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。[[欽明天皇]]の[[皇子]]。[[聖徳太子]]の叔父でもある。表記は『[[日本書紀]]』によるが、『[[古事記]]』では三枝部穴太部王（さきくさべのあなほべのみこ）、別名を須売伊呂杼（すめいろど（[[皇后]]の同母弟か））とする。『日本書紀』[[欽明天皇]]紀では住迹（すみと（[[天皇]]の弟か））皇子、[[用明天皇]]紀では皇弟（すめいろど）皇子とも記している。
== 生涯 == 欽明天皇の[[皇子]]として誕生。母は[[蘇我稲目]]の娘・[[蘇我小姉君|小姉君]]。異母兄に[[敏達天皇]]と[[用明天皇]]など。異母姉に[[推古天皇]]。同母姉に[[穴穂部間人皇女]]（用明天皇妃、[[聖徳太子]]生母）、同母弟に[[崇峻天皇]]、同母兄に[[茨城皇子]]と[[葛城皇子]]。
穴穂部皇子は皇位を望んでおり、敏達天皇14年（[[585年]]）8月、兄・敏達天皇が[[崩御]]し、[[殯|殯宮]]で[[葬儀]]が行われた際に「何故に死する王に仕え、生きる王（自分）に仕えないのか」と憤慨した<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇14年8月15日条</ref>。ところが、同年9月に[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]・[[蘇我馬子]]の推す大兄皇子（[[用明天皇]]）が即位したため、これに対抗し[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]・[[物部守屋]]と結んだ。
用明天皇元年（[[586年]]）5月、炊屋姫（敏達天皇の皇后、後の[[推古天皇]]）を犯さんと欲し、殯宮に押し入ろうとした。これに対し先帝の寵臣[[三輪逆]]は門を閉じて拒み、穴穂部皇子は7度門前で呼んだが、遂に宮に入ることができなかった。穴穂部皇子は蘇我馬子と物部守屋に三輪逆は不遜であると相談し、馬子らはこれに同意。守屋は兵を率い（用明天皇の宮のある）磐余の池辺の地で三輪逆を包囲するが、三輪逆は逃れて炊屋姫の後宮に隠れた。しかし、密告により居所を知ると穴穂部皇子は三輪逆とその二人の子を殺すよう守屋に命じ、守屋は兵を率いて向かった。その後、報告を聞こうと守屋のもとへ赴こうとするが、これを知りかけつけた馬子と門前で出会い「王者は刑人に近づくべからず」と諫言されるが、穴穂部皇子は聞き入れようとしなかった。馬子は仕方なくついて行き、磐余に至ったところで再度諫言、穴穂部皇子もこれに従い、胡床に座り守屋を待ち、戻ってきた守屋から三輪逆を斬ったと報告を受けた（或本では、穴穂部皇子自身が射殺した、とある）。なお、馬子は「天下の乱は近い」と嘆くが、守屋は「汝のような小臣の知るところにあらず」と答えている<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇元年5月条</ref>。
用明天皇2年（[[587年]]）4月2日、用明天皇は病になり[[仏法]]を信奉したいと欲し群臣に諮った。排仏派の守屋は反対したが、崇仏派の馬子は詔を奉じて助けるべきとして、穴穂部皇子に[[僧]]の[[豊国 (僧)|豊国]]を連れて来させた<ref>『日本書紀』ではこの場面のみ穴穂部皇子を「皇弟（すめいろど）皇子」と記している。同じ名前は[[孝徳天皇]]の時代、皇后[[間人皇女]]の同母弟だった大海人皇子（後の[[天武天皇]]）にも用いられている。法師については「名をもらせり」と注しており、人名ではなく[[豊国]]の法師の意と見られる。</ref>。守屋は自分が推していた穴穂部皇子が[[法師]]を連れてきたことに大いに怒り睨みつけた。その後、守屋は群臣から命を狙われていると知らされて、別業の阿都（[[河内国]]）へ退いた。
同年4月9日、用明天皇は崩御した<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年4月9日条</ref>が、後嗣が定まらず皇位は一時的に空位となった。そのため同年5月、守屋は穴穂部皇子を天皇に立てようと思い、密使を皇子に送り、遊猟に出ると欺いて淡路へ来るよう願った<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年5月条</ref>。これに対し同年6月7日、蘇我馬子は炊屋姫を奉じて、[[佐伯丹経手|佐伯連丹経手]]、[[土師磐村|土師連磐村]]、[[的真噛|的臣真噛]]に速やかに穴穂部皇子と[[宅部皇子]]を誅殺するよう命じた。その日の夜半、佐伯連丹經手らは穴穂部皇子の宮を囲んだ。穴穂部皇子は楼を登ってきた衛士に肩を斬られると、楼から落ちて隣家へ走り入ったが、灯をかかげた衞士らによって探し出され殺害された<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年6月7日条</ref>。なお、翌8日には穴穂部皇子と仲が良かった宅部皇子も誅殺され<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年6月8日条</ref>、同年7月には馬子によって物部守屋も滅ぼされている<ref>『日本書紀』用明天皇2年7月条</ref>。
== 登場作品 == * テレビドラマ ** [[蘇我馬子|怒涛日本史 蘇我馬子の陰謀]]（[[1966年]] [[毎日放送]] 演：[[仲谷昇]]） ** [[聖徳太子 (テレビドラマ)|聖徳太子]]（[[2001年]] [[日本放送協会|NHK]] 演：[[柄本明]]） * 漫画 ** [[日出処の天子]]（[[山岸凉子]]） ** 聖徳太子（[[池田理代子]]）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[宇治谷孟]]『日本書紀 （下）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1988年 * 『日本書紀』（四）（[[岩波文庫]]、1995年）
== 関連項目 == * [[丁未の乱]] * [[穴穂部]]

revision.text: [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|qid=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|qid=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|qid=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|qid=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|qid=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|qid=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|qid=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|qid=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|qid=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|qid=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|qid=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|qq=|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}}

[[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|qq=|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}}

[[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}}

The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
