This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597248]] Wikidata name: Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1="Yet the Mononobe sent for him to go "Fishing", this was an attempt at making him Emperor. This failed." what the hell is that??? 03:43, 17 July 2024 (UTC)}} {{AfC comment|1=The footnotes should be placed throughout the article. [[User:GoingBatty|GoingBatty]] ([[User talk:GoingBatty|talk]]) 14:11, 31 October 2023 (UTC)}} {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]] | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]], the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to [[:en:Awaji Province|Awaji Province]], pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the [[:en:Soga–Mononobe conflict|Soga–Mononobe conflict]] due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] |6= 6. [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] |7= 7. [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |15=15. [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *[[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] *[[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] *[[:en:Soga no Emishi|Soga no Emishi]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} |6= 6. {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} |7= 7. {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka}} |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} |15=15. {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}} }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *{{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} *{{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}} *{{ill|Soga no Emishi|en|Soga no Emishi|WD=Q1292368|lt=Soga no Emishi}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province}} | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Princess Nukatabe}}, the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province}}, pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} |6= 6. {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} |7= 7. {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka}} |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} |15=15. {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}} }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *{{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} *{{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}} *{{ill|Soga no Emishi|en|Soga no Emishi|WD=Q1292368|lt=Soga no Emishi}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}} wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province}}, pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} |6= 6. {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} |7= 7. {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka}} |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikōshi no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} |15=15. {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}} }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *{{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} *{{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}} *{{ill|Soga no Emishi|en|Soga no Emishi|WD=Q1292368|lt=Soga no Emishi}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text: [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}} | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199|1=Oane-no-kimi}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250|1=Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775|1=Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769|1=Imperial Prince Iso no Kami}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913|1=Imperial Prince Katsuraki}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858|1=Imperial Prince Sakurai}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238|1=Emperor Sushun}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281|1=Emperor Yōmei}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Princess Nukatabe|1=Empress Suiko}}, the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Soga–Mononobe conflict|1=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2. [[Oanenokimi]] |3= 3. {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} |6= 6. {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} |7= 7. {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Tashiraka|1=Princess Tashiraka}} |12=12. {{ill|Hikoushi no Ōkimi|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoushi no Ōkimi|1=Hikoushi no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487892}} |13=13. Furihime |14=14. {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}} |15=15. {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|1=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}} }} == See also == {{Portal|Japan}} *[[Soga clan]] *[[Mononobe clan]] *{{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} *{{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}} *{{ill|Soga no Emishi|en|Soga no Emishi|WD=Q1292368|lt=Soga no Emishi|1=Soga no Emishi}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Drafts moved from mainspace|date=October 2023}} {{translated page|ja|穴穂部皇子|version=104188720|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[user:Lemonaka|-Lemonaka]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]] [[ru:%D0%90%D0%BD%D0%B0%D1%85%D0%BE%D0%B1%D1%8D]] [[ko:%EC%95%84%EB%82%98%ED%98%B8%EB%B2%A0%EB%85%B8%EB%AF%B8%EC%BD%94]] [[ko:아나호베노미코]] [[ru:Анахобэ]] {{wikidata link|Q11597248}} [[Category:587 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Assassinated Members of the Japanese Imperial Family]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kimmei]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Asuka period]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] [[ja:穴穂部皇子]][[ko:아나호베노미코]][[ru:Анахобэ]] {{AFC submission|d|bio|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Thilsebatti|declinets=20241103054525|ts=20241001145523}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Asilvering|declinets=20240820231554|small=yes|ts=20240814102234}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=SafariScribe|declinets=20240801051117|small=yes|ts=20240718132634}} {{AFC submission|d|context|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=Lemonaka|declinets=20240717034301|small=yes|ts=20240331213138}} {{AFC submission|d|v|u=Camillz|ns=118|decliner=GoingBatty|declinets=20231031141102|small=yes|ts=20231030151305}} {{AFC submission|d|essay|u=Camillz‬|ns=118|decliner=Utopes|declinets=20231012055721|small=yes|ts=20231011170607}} ---- {{Short description|Japanese Imperial Prince during the Asuka period.}} {{Draft topics|biography|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|bdp}} {{Infobox person | name = Prince Anahobe | birth_date = 0550 | birth_place = [[Asuka]] | death_date = {{death date|0587|7|17}} | death_place = {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}} | title = Imperial Prince | mother = {{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199|1=Oane-no-kimi}} | father = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | relatives= {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|WD=Q11597250|1=Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}}; {{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775|1=Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769|1=Imperial Prince Iso no Kami}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913|1=Imperial Prince Katsuraki}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858|1=Imperial Prince Sakurai}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238|1=Emperor Sushun}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281|1=Emperor Yōmei}} }} Prince Anahobe (穴穂部皇子) was a Japanese Prince. He was born to Emperor Kinmei and a consort Oanenokimi, Anahobe would have been titled "Imperial Prince Anahobe"{{Cite book |last=William George |first=Aston |author-link=William George Aston |url=https://en.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697 |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} == Life == Not much is known about his early life. His life is mainly recorded from 585 onward when [[Emperor Bidatsu]] died. He was recorded saying, 'I don't understand why you obey the dead king and not the living one.'He wouldn't become Emperor despite effort as in October 585 [[:en:Emperor Yomei|Prince Ōe]], would become the Emperor much to the prince's dismay. In May 586, Prince Anahobe tried to break into Hinkyu to rape {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Princess Nukatabe|1=Empress Suiko}}, the widow of Emperor Bidatsu. He and many others described her as, 'fair of face and figure; well-mannered and polite.' As Anahobe tried break in, [[Miwa no Sakau]], a favorite retainer of Emperor Bidatsu refused to let him in closing all the gates. Although Prince Anahobe called seven times at the gate, he still couldn't enter Hinkyu. He did complain to [[Soga no Umako]] stating Sakau was useless, this was an attempt to get him fired so that he could get to Nukatabe, Although this didn't work.{{Cite web |date=2023-05-06 |title=Soga Clan (蘇我氏) |url=https://jref.com/articles/soga-clan-%E8%98%87%E6%88%91%E6%B0%8F.646/ |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Japan Reference |language=en-GB}} When Emperor Yomei was dying he wondered wether to practice Buddhism or not. While the [[Mononobe clan]] was anti Buddhist, [[Soga no Umako]] was pro Buddhist. He sent for Anahobe to get a Buddhist Priest named [[Toyokuni (Buddhist preist)|Toyokuni]].{{Cite web |title=The Story of Nun Zenshin-ni |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/zenshinni/life/p3.html |access-date=2023-10-02 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}} In May 587, {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} wanted to make Prince Anahobe the Emperor. This was due to Ōe's death. Moriya believed that Anahobe would be on the Shinto side. So he sent an agent to Prince Anahobe and asked him to come to {{ill|Awaji Province|en|Awaji Province|WD=Q1053717|lt=Awaji Province|1=Awaji Province}}, pretending to go out for hunting. Soga no Umako caught wind of this. This would end the {{ill|Soga–Mononobe conflict|en|Soga–Mononobe conflict|WD=Q534720|lt=Soga–Mononobe conflict|1=Soga–Mononobe conflict}} due to Umako sending assassins to kill Moriya, Prince Anahobe, and [[Prince Yakabe]] an ally of Anahobe. When Moriya died Anahobe was defenceless. The assassin sent to kill Anahobe, climbed the tower Anahobe was on and cut his shoulder, Anahobe fell and died. The next day Yakabe also was killed.Takeshi Umehara , ''仏教の勝利'' (Tokyo: Shogakkan, 1980), 291-292. == Soga & Mononobe clans == The clans of Mononobe and Soga attempted to win him over. Both wanted to make him Emperor. Soga no Umako sent Anahobe to get the Buddhist priest as a way to (in his eyes) possibly convert Anahobe to {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} . The Mononobe then sent a request to prince Anahobe, to go "Fishing", this was an attempt to secretly crown and proclaim him as emperor. This failed. ==Ancestry== {{ahnentafel |collapsed=yes |align=center |boxstyle_1=background-color: #fcc; |boxstyle_2=background-color: #fb9; |boxstyle_3=background-color: #ffc; |boxstyle_4=background-color: #bfc; |boxstyle_5=background-color: #9fe; |ref={{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |date=1896 |publisher=Society |isbn=978-0-524-05347-8 |language=en}} |1= 1. '''Prince Anahobe''' |2= 2.
